success_surface: Complex multi-step spreadsheet manipulation requiring data analysis, conditional value updates, selective formatting removal, and targeted border re-application with exception logic
effective_behaviors: Explored complete spreadsheet structure programmatically before implementing any changes
Used mixed library approach: pandas for data analysis/aggregation, openpyxl for formatting operations
Separated concerns into distinct sequential steps: analysis → data update → formatting clear → selective formatting reapply
Created exception tracking set for conditional formatting rules (exempt specific cells from style changes)
Verified index conversions between Python (0-indexed) and Excel (1-indexed) when accessing cells
Saved modified workbook to new output path without altering original file
Logged detailed execution metrics (cells updated, formatting changes applied) in final report
evidence: Agent ran comprehensive exploration code listing sheet names, dimensions, non-empty cells, and DataFrame shape before solution generation
Loaded data into pandas for Counter() combination counting while keeping openpyxl workbook object for style manipulation
Implemented 4 clear phases: duplicate detection via Counter(), Remark column updates, fill/border clearance iteration, conditional border reapplication with border_exceptions set
Used range(4, df.shape[0]) and ws.cell(row=row_idx + 1, column=...) to handle 0→1 index conversion correctly across 11 data rows
Output path '/mnt/data/outputs/...' saved new file; print statements reported 11 remark cells updated, 22 borders applied
Removed all cell.fill and cell.border, then selectively re-added thin_border only to populated cells not in exception set

memory_items.title: Pre-Implementation Structure Discovery
memory_items.lesson: Always programmatically explore sheet names, dimensions, and cell contents before implementing transformations.
memory_items.generalization: Run discovery code (wb.sheetnames, df.shape, iter_rows with None checks) to identify actual data ranges before writing modification logic.

memory_items.title: Dual-Library Workflow Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Separate heavy data processing from Excel formatting operations by leveraging both pandas and openpyxl.
memory_items.generalization: Use pandas for aggregation, filtering, counting, or complex calculations; use openpyxl only for cell-level styling, formula management, and workbook saving.

memory_items.title: Two-Pass Formatting Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Clear all existing formatting before reapplying styles conditionally to ensure consistent visual output.
memory_items.generalization: When reformatting a range, first iterate through all cells to reset fills/borders, then second pass selectively applies styles based on conditions.

memory_items.title: Conditional Formatting Exception Sets
memory_items.lesson: Track cells requiring special treatment using a dedicated collection before applying bulk formatting.
memory_items.generalization: Build exception collections (sets/lists) identifying cells to exclude from common formatting rules, then check membership during the apply-pass.

memory_items.title: Index Conversion Verification
memory_items.lesson: Explicitly handle the offset between Python array indices (0-based) and Excel cell references (1-based).
memory_items.generalization: When reading from pandas.DataFrame, add 1 to row index before passing to ws.cell(); document this offset in code comments.

memory_items.title: Execution Metric Reporting
memory_items.lesson: Report concrete counts of modified cells, applied formats, and skipped entries for validation and debugging.
memory_items.generalization: Log summary statistics (rows processed, cells changed, formatting applications, exceptions hit) after completing spreadsheet manipulation tasks.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Exploration First**: Always run discovery code (wb.sheetnames, df.shape, iter_rows) to map actual data ranges before implementing transformation logic.
- **Dual-Library Workflow**: Use pandas for aggregation/counting/filtering; reserve openpyxl exclusively for styling, formulas, and workbook I/O operations.
- **Format Reset-and-Reapply Pattern**: When changing formatting, first clear all fills/borders in a full range pass, then selectively reapply in a second pass based on conditions.
- **Exception Collection Pattern**: Build sets/lists of cells requiring special formatting treatment, then filter against these collections during bulk style application.
- **Index Offset Discipline**: Add 1 to pandas row indices when calling ws.cell(); explicitly comment this 0→1 conversion at decision points.
- **Metric Documentation**: Log execution statistics (rows processed, cells changed, format applications, skipped entries) to enable verification and debugging.
